phrases.cultural_note: The Vauban system includes several covered bridges (ponts couverts). They were designed to protect the city from attacks.

responses.english: Right next to the dam, you can see them from here.
responses.french: Juste à côté du barrage, vous les voyez d'ici.

phrases.intent_en: How do we get up to the terrace?
phrases.cultural_note: The covered terrace walkway is the main reason most visitors stop here.

responses.english: By the staircase inside, on the left.
responses.french: Par l'escalier à l'intérieur, sur la gauche.

phrases.intent_en: Is the panoramic terrace open today?
phrases.text_local: La terrasse panoramique est ouverte aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah te-RASS pah-noh-rah-MEEK eh oo-VAIRT oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: Access can change with maintenance hours or weather, so it is worth confirming on site.

responses.english: Yes, free access. Go up by the stairs.
responses.phonetic: wee, say tun pwahn an-por-TANT duh lees-TWAR loh-KAL
responses.french: Oui, en accès libre. Montez par l'escalier.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the rooftop terrace is open
phrases.cultural_note: The Barrage Vauban is a 17th-century weir designed by Vauban that offers a panoramic terrace over the petite France quarter and the covered bridges.

responses.english: Yes, access is free and open all day.
responses.phonetic: wee, lak-SAY ay grah-TWEET ay LEEBR toot lah zhoor-NAY.
responses.french: Oui, l'accès est gratuit et libre toute la journée.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Ponts Couverts are from here
phrases.text_local: Où se trouvent les Ponts Couverts depuis le barrage ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lay PON koo-VAIR duh-PWEE luh bah-RAHZH

responses.english: Right in front, they're perfectly visible from here.
responses.phonetic: zhoos tahn FAHS, on lay vwah par-FAY-tuh-MAHN dee-SEE.
responses.french: Juste en face, on les voit parfaitement d'ici.
